name: mill-lumber
summary: A water- or ox-driven sawmill. Massive pit-saw or circular blade, rough logs feeding in one side, finished boards stacked on the other. Sawdust everywhere.

size_guidance.aspect_ratio: long rectangle aligned with the saw carriage

accent.purpose: sawdust accumulation under and around the blade

primary_palette.note: pit-saw mill — two-man reciprocating blade, log fed from above
water-driven circular saw — a proud modern mill
production mill — bulk board output, carriage mid-cut

story_prompts: full production — logs queuing at the inlet, boards stacking at the outlet, shouting over the noise
blade-broken — saw stopped, sawyer cursing at a snapped tooth
after hours — mill silent, one figure sharpening the blade by lantern-light
flood surge — water wheel running wild, sawyer scrambling to brake the carriage

lighting_notes.ambient_intent: bright working light (0.4-0.5) — often half-open to the elements

anti_patterns: don't center the blade — put it along the long axis so the log carriage has room to travel
logs enter one end, boards leave the other — enforce the flow direction visually
don't block the drive shaft path from the exterior wall
